layout.title: Argos – Website auditor
layout.description: Argos scans HTTP headers, HTML quality, tech stack and Lighthouse performance. Scored, private report - no login required.

hero.headline:
Find what's hurting your site.
Free, no account, in 30 seconds.
hero.sub: HTTP, SEO, performance, stack - Argos analyzes everything and gives you a prioritized report with the concrete actions to take first.
hero.audience: For developers, growth teams, and agencies.
hero.hint: Free · No account · Private report, not indexed
hero.errorMsg: Error: check the URL and try again.
hero.errorFailed: Analysis failed. Try with a public URL.

modules.title: What Argos analyzes
modules.sub: Five independent modules. One complete picture.

items.label: HTTP & Security
items.desc: Status code, redirects, HTTPS enforcement, HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, compression…

items.label: HTML & SEO
items.desc: Title tag, meta description, canonical, H1, lang attribute, viewport, OpenGraph, Twitter Card, image alt…

items.label: Tech stack detection
items.desc: CMS (WordPress, Shopify, Wix…), frameworks (Next.js, Nuxt, React, Vue…), backend hints, Cloudflare CDN.

items.label: Playwright runtime
items.desc: Metrics collected on a rendered page: console errors, LCP, FCP, TTI, render-blocking resources.

items.label: Lighthouse
items.desc: Performance, accessibility, best practices and SEO scores via headless Lighthouse.

steps.label: Submit a URL
steps.desc: Any public site. Paste the URL and launch the analysis in one click.

steps.label: We analyze everything
steps.desc: Five modules run in sequence: HTTP → HTML → Tech → Runtime → Lighthouse.

steps.label: Scored report
steps.desc: A private (non-indexable) report with a global score and actionable recommendations.

items.label: Private by default
items.desc: Each report is accessed via a unique token. No index, no SEO.

items.label: No login required
items.desc: No account, no tracking. Submit a URL, get your report.

items.label: Actionable report
items.desc: Every detected issue is prioritized by real impact. You know exactly what to fix first - without reading everything.

items.label: Actionable scoring
items.desc: Every detected issue is weighted, prioritized and paired with a concrete recommendation - not just a number.

cta.title: Ready to see what's hiding in your site?
cta.sub: Free · No account · Private report · Open source

meta.title: FAQ – Frequently asked questions
meta.description: Answers to common questions about Argos: how it works, reports, analysis limits, privacy and what's coming next.

hero.title: Frequently asked questions
hero.sub: Everything you need to know about Argos, reports, analysis limits and how your data is handled.

items.a: Argos is a website audit tool: you submit a URL, five analysis modules run in sequence (HTTP, HTML, performance, Lighthouse, security) and you get a scored, prioritized report with concrete actions to take.
Yes. Analysis is completely free, with no account to create and no credit card required.

items.q: How long does an analysis take?
items.a: A full analysis takes about 20 to 30 seconds. The report page updates automatically once the audit is complete.
Any public site reachable over HTTP or HTTPS. For security reasons, local or private addresses (localhost, internal networks) are blocked.
An overall score, detailed scores per category (performance, security, SEO, accessibility) and a list of prioritized issues, sorted by severity, with recommended actions.
No. Each report is accessible through a URL containing a unique token. It is not indexed by search engines and stays private unless you share the link.
Yes. Just send the report link: anyone with the link can view it. Only share it with people you trust.

items.q: How long does a report stay available?
items.a: Reports have an expiration date. After that, the link is no longer valid and you need to run a new analysis to get an up-to-date report.

Five modules: HTTP headers, HTML structure and SEO, runtime metrics (perceived performance), Lighthouse scores (performance, accessibility, best practices, SEO) and security (SSL, security headers, basic vulnerability scan).

items.q: Does Argos replace a manual audit?
items.a: No. Argos gives a fast, prioritized automated diagnosis. It does not replace the expertise of an in-depth audit performed by a specialist.
Most often because the site is unreachable, too slow to respond, or blocks bots. Check that the URL is correct and publicly accessible, then try again.

items.q: Is there a limit on the number of analyses?
items.a: There is no strict quota for normal use. Reasonable usage is expected to keep the service fast and reliable for everyone.
Argos keeps the analyzed URL and the generated report for as long as needed to display it. No personal data is required to run an analysis.
Only people who have the link containing the report token. Reports are neither listed publicly nor indexed.

items.q: Do my reports show up in Google?
items.a: No. Report pages are marked as non-indexable and protected by a token, so they don't appear in search results.
Basic analysis is meant to stay free. Advanced features may be offered later as part of a dedicated plan.
Ideas under consideration include tracking score changes over time, comparing analyses, exporting reports and API access. Nothing is set in stone yet.

items.q: How can I stay informed about new features?
items.a: You can subscribe to the newsletter from the home page to be notified about updates and new features.

stillQuestions.title: Still have a question?
stillQuestions.sub: The easiest way is often to try it. Run a free analysis of your site.

error.title: Oops, something went wrong
error.description: An unexpected error occurred on our side. You can try again or go back home.

dataTab.title: Report JSON
dataTab.desc: Useful for debugging and for the upcoming PDF export.

hero.scoreTooltip: Aggregated score (0–100) based on detected issues.

oneLiner.high: Solid site: a few optimizations could further improve impact.
oneLiner.good: Good potential: targeted actions can improve performance and conversion.
oneLiner.fair: Several blockers: a small roadmap can quickly raise the score.
oneLiner.low: Big opportunities: fixing critical issues will improve trust and conversion.

footerCta.title: Schedule a call
footerCta.desc: 15 minutes to prioritize optimizations, estimate effort, and identify quick wins.

priorityCards.title: Recommended priorities
priorityCards.desc: High-impact quick wins (performance / security / quality) with reasonable effort.
priorityCards.effortTooltip: Indicative estimate (XS → L). Adjust based on project context.

scoreGrid.title: Scores by category
scoreGrid.desc: Synthetic view for prioritization. Click a category to see details.
scoreGrid.globalTooltip: Aggregated global score (0–100) based on detected issues.

issuesByCategory.title: Details by category
issuesByCategory.desc: Filter by severity, then explore issues by category (sorted critical → info).
